title: Stage 4 Phase 1: Clip bounds BINDING (multi-K exact-spec validation)

content.hypothesis: Entry 039 (single-K K=6 diagnostic) concluded clip bounds are non-binding. This was methodologically limited by CV≈14.8%. Using multi-K averaging K=[4,6,8,10] which achieves CV≈5.1% (3x reduction), the same clip bounds conditions should be reanalyzed to test if binding effects are revealed.
content.method: Multi-K reanalysis of Task #14 clip bounds sweep. Average MI gaps across K=[4,6,8,10], paired seeds same as original. Same conditions: CANONICAL [0.3,1.8], VERY_NARROW [0.7,1.3], NARROW_TIGHT [0.5,1.6], NARROW_LO [0.5,1.8], WIDE_LO [0.1,1.8], ASYMM_LO [0.05,1.8], ASYMM_HI [0.3,2.8], WIDE_HI [0.3,2.8].
content.result:
PASSED. Binding effects CONFIRMED at multi-K resolution.

Key numbers:
- Canonical [0.3, 1.8]: mean MI gap = +0.6274, CV=5.1%
- VERY_NARROW [0.7, 1.3]: d=7.583, p=0.000 (highly significant)
- NARROW_TIGHT [0.5, 1.6]: d=2.841, p=0.001 (significant)
- NARROW_LO [0.5, 1.8]: d=1.253, p=0.091 (borderline)
- All other conditions: d<0.5, p>0.1 (non-binding)

Conclusion: Clip bounds ARE BINDING when tested with adequate power (multi-K CV=5.1%). The single-K conclusion (p=0.096, non-binding) was a methodological false negative due to high variance.
content.methodology_lesson: The earlier Task #14 conclusion was undermined by single-K variance (CV=14.8%). At n=5 paired seeds, d=0.610 is in the noisy zone (insufficient to overcome natural sampling variance). Multi-K averaging moves the signal into the clear zone (d=7.583, p=0.000). This is NOT a contradiction; it's a power issue.
content.implication: Stage 4 Phase 2 must test CLIP BOUNDS as a PRIMARY binding target, not a secondary candidate. VERY_NARROW [0.7,1.3] has the strongest effect size of any plasticity parameter tested to date (d=7.583, exceeds tau d=-0.851). This should take priority over state parameters for Phase 2.
content.frozen_frame_accounting: Clip bounds become per-cell adaptive: alpha_clip_lo[NC], alpha_clip_hi[NC]. New frozen elements: clip_signal (how to drive adaptation), clip_meta (meta-rate). Net change: +2 frozen elements (justified as necessary infrastructure).
content.priority_ranking:
Stage 4 Phase 2 priority (updated):
1. TIER 0 (PRIMARY): Clip bounds VERY_NARROW [0.7,1.3] — d=7.583, strongest binding
2. TIER 1: State param eps — d=-0.663, secondary binding
3. TIER 2: State param delta — d=-0.502
4. TIER 3: State param tau — d=-0.851 (strongest absolute but harder signal design)

constraints_implied: Single-K diagnostics insufficient; multi-K protocol required (K=[4,6,8,10] averaging achieves CV≈5.1% vs K=6-only CV≈14.8%)
